pages.raw_text:
WALLACE. Idaho. Feb. 11.-A
wreck in which seven men were in
jured took place on the Missoula
branch of the Northern Pacific. ii
miles east of here. yesterday morn
ing. The 50 bridge, on which a
passenger coach, engine and caboose
were standing, was swept away by a
snowslide.


The engine and caboose We're
thrown into the gulch rs feet below,
while the passenger coach was left
hanging over the end of the broken
bridge.


The seven railroad men who were
all seriously injured are;


j. G. Phillips, engineer, injured
about the hips and body, nose broken
and cut about face.


A. Satersmoen. fireman, serious hip
and spinal injury.


C. Smith, conductor, cut about the
face.


f. f. Rice, fireman, injured hips.


Fred Grant, brakeman, badly bruis-
ed about the body


W. j. Merritt, brakeman. badly
shaken up and broken arm.


Brakeman Maxwell badly bruised.


Phillips and Satersmoen are in the
hospital here. The others were taken
to their homes
